skill.name: firebase-security-rules-auditor
skill.description: Audits Firebase Firestore security rules for access control gaps, validation bypasses, privilege risks, and business logic mismatches.
skill.summary: Reviews Firestore rules with a red-team checklist for secure access control and validation.
skill.author: firebase

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command, weak cryptography, and system reconnaissance patterns in SKILL.md. Review found these are documentation-only false positives: Markdown code spans, checklist wording, and output schema field names. No executable code, prompt injection, network access, filesystem access, or confirmed malicious behavior was found.

low_findings.title: Static Finding Dismissed: Markdown Code Spans
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick execution at SKILL.md line 19 is Markdown formatting around Firestore rule helper names. It is not executable code and does not invoke a shell.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 19 is checklist prose containing inline Markdown code spans. There is no script, interpreter directive, or command execution context.

low_findings.title: Static Finding Dismissed: Weak Cryptography Keyword Match
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts at SKILL.md lines 3 and 41 are false positives from security-related text and an output field named issue. No cryptographic algorithm is referenced or used.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines contain descriptive skill metadata and a reporting schema field. No MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or comparable weak algorithm appears in context.

low_findings.title: Static Finding Dismissed: Reconnaissance Keyword Match
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance alerts at SKILL.md lines 14 and 19 are false positives from audit checklist language. The file does not request host inspection or environment discovery.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines discuss Firestore update and ownership checks. No commands such as whoami, uname, id, env, or filesystem enumeration are present.

content.value_statement: Weak Firestore rules can expose data or block valid product workflows. This skill reviews rules for bypasses, ownership gaps, validation issues, and privilege risks.
actual_capabilities: Reviews Firestore rules against a structured security checklist.
Checks create and update paths for validation bypasses.
Identifies ownership and role authority weaknesses.
Flags missing type checks and size limits.
Rates findings with a simple one-to-five security score.
Returns recommendations for each reported rules issue.
limitations: Focuses on Firestore security rules, not all Firebase products.
Requires the user to provide the relevant rules and app context.
Does not execute tests against a live Firebase project.
Cannot prove business requirements without accurate product details.

use_cases.title: Pre-Release Rules Review
use_cases.description: Review new Firestore rules before deploying a feature that stores user or team data.

use_cases.title: Security Regression Check
use_cases.description: Compare changed rules with expected ownership, role, and validation behavior after a refactor.

use_cases.title: Product Logic Validation
use_cases.description: Confirm rules support collaboration, admin access, and user workflows without unsafe workarounds.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Rules Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit these Firestore security rules for obvious access control and validation issues. Summarize the score, risks, and fixes.
prompt_templates.scenario: A beginner wants a quick security review before deployment.

prompt_templates.title: Create and Update Bypass Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Check whether users can create a valid document and later update it into an invalid or privileged state.
prompt_templates.scenario: A developer changed validation logic and needs targeted review.

prompt_templates.title: Ownership and Role Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these rules for ownership checks, role authority sources, admin bootstrapping, and cross-user write risks.
prompt_templates.scenario: A team stores user profiles, roles, or shared workspace data.

prompt_templates.title: Full Red-Team Assessment
prompt_templates.prompt: Act as a Firestore security auditor. Find bypass paths, broken business logic, type gaps, size-limit risks, and privilege escalation paths.
prompt_templates.scenario: An advanced user wants a comprehensive pre-launch assessment.

output_examples.input: User profile rules allow authenticated users to edit profile fields.
output_examples.output: The assessment reports a major issue if any authenticated user can update another profile. It recommends adding strict owner checks.

output_examples.input: Team documents use role fields supplied by the submitted document.
output_examples.output: The review flags self-assigned authority. It recommends deriving roles from trusted existing data or server-managed membership records.

output_examples.input: Rules validate required fields on create but not on update.
output_examples.output: The result highlights an update bypass. It recommends applying equivalent type, ownership, and size checks on every write path.

best_practices: Provide the complete Firestore rules file and key app workflows.
Include examples of user roles, document ownership, and collaboration models.
Ask for bypass paths and concrete remediation steps, not only a score.
anti_patterns: Do not assume field restrictions replace ownership checks.
Do not rely on user-submitted role or admin fields for authority.
Do not review isolated rule snippets without explaining product behavior.

faq.question: What does this skill audit?
faq.answer: It audits Firestore security rules for access control, validation, role authority, update bypasses, and resource abuse risks.

faq.question: Does it support Firebase Storage rules?
faq.answer: The skill is written for Firestore rules. Storage rules may need a different review checklist.

faq.question: Can it test a live Firebase project?
faq.answer: No. It reviews provided rules and context, but it does not run emulator or production tests.

faq.question: What should I include in a prompt?
faq.answer: Include the rules, document structure, user roles, ownership model, and the workflows the app must support.

faq.question: How are results scored?
faq.answer: The skill uses a one-to-five scale where one means critical risk and five means strong rules coverage.

faq.question: Can it find business logic issues?
faq.answer: Yes, when the prompt explains the app purpose and expected user permissions.
